source_notes: Compiled from JEES JLPT N5 specification (post-2010), official sample papers, Genki/Minna teaching notes, and observed N5 trap patterns across this module's 178 grammar + 1009 vocab + 106 kanji + 54 dokkai + 50 chokai items.

section_1.name: Language Knowledge (Kanji-Vocab) + Language Knowledge (Grammar) + Reading
section_1.alt_name: 言語知識（文字・語彙）＋（文法）＋読解

exam_structure.section_minimums_rule: Must score >=38 in combined Lang Knowledge + Reading AND >=19 in Listening. Total >=80.

mondai_breakdown.strategy: Don't overthink — first reading you recognize is usually right. Scan distractors for slight kana variations (-こう / -ごう).
Match kana given against correct kanji. Watch for homophone distractors (はやい 速い vs 早い).
Read the entire blank-sentence; let context narrow choice. Common trap: same POS but wrong nuance.
Identify the key meaning of the underlined word; match to synonym. Watch for partial-overlap distractors.
Word given — pick the sentence using it correctly. Eliminate by collocation/particle mismatches.

moji_goi.time_budget_warning: If stuck >2x normal time on a single item, mark and move on; come back at end.

mondai_breakdown.strategy: Identify what slot needs filling (particle / verb form / conjunction). Eliminate based on form rules first.
Star (★) marks the asked slot. Build the sentence mentally first; check what fills the star. Don't assemble all 4 pieces in order — find the structurally-forced order.
Connectives and discourse markers. Read the whole paragraph first; context disambiguates.
Short passage (~50-150 chars). Read once for gist, then scan for the answer keyword.
Medium passage (~200-300 chars). Read once carefully; questions usually target main idea + one detail.
Notice/schedule/menu format. SCAN for the specific information; do not read linearly.

bunpou_dokkai.time_budget_warning: Reserve 10 min minimum for the final 3 questions (long reading + info search).

chokai.format_note: Audio is played ONCE per item. Read prompt/choices BEFORE audio if time allows.

mondai_breakdown.strategy: Question read FIRST (printed). Listen for the SPEAKER'S FINAL DECISION/TASK.
Question read FIRST (printed). Listen for the SPECIFIC INFORMATION asked.
Visual prompt shows situation. Pick the natural utterance for that context. Match register to relationship.
Hear utterance; pick natural response. NO TIME to think — answer by feel. Common trap: similar-sound responses.

chokai.rest_break: There is NO break between mondai. Maintain focus throughout.

trap_patterns.name: particle_ha_vs_ga_question_words
trap_patterns.description: Question words (だれ/なに/どこ/いつ) take が, never は.
trap_patterns.wrong_example: だれは きましたか。
trap_patterns.correct_example: だれが きましたか。
trap_patterns.defense: Memorize: Q-word → が. は cannot topicalize an unknown.

trap_patterns.name: ga_for_subject_of_feeling_adj
trap_patterns.description: Feeling/skill adjectives (すき/きらい/上手/下手/ほしい/わかる/できる) take が for their object.
trap_patterns.wrong_example: コーヒーを すきです。
trap_patterns.correct_example: コーヒーが すきです。
trap_patterns.defense: Memorize: 〜が adj. category. Same applies to ほしい/たい.

trap_patterns.name: te_form_irregular_iku
trap_patterns.description: 行く is the only Group-1 verb with っ-promotion in te-form (行って, NOT 行きて).
trap_patterns.wrong_example: がっこうに いきて、たべます。
trap_patterns.correct_example: がっこうに いって、たべます。
trap_patterns.defense: Memorize the 6 G1 exception verbs: 行く 帰る 走る 知る 切る 要る.

trap_patterns.name: iadj_negative_drop_i
trap_patterns.description: い-Adj negative drops final い: 高い→高くない. NOT 高いくない.
trap_patterns.defense: い→く transformation; only いい is irregular (よくない).

trap_patterns.name: naadj_negative_jaarimasen
trap_patterns.description: na-Adj negative uses じゃありません, NOT くない. きれい LOOKS like い-Adj but is na-Adj.
trap_patterns.correct_example: きれいじゃありません。
trap_patterns.defense: Remember na-Adj list: きれい, ゆうめい, しずか, げんき, etc.

trap_patterns.name: relative_time_no_particle
trap_patterns.description: Relative time-words (きょう/あした/きのう/まいにち/らいしゅう) take NO particle.
trap_patterns.defense: Only specific clock-time / date takes に.

trap_patterns.name: humble_respectful_inversion
trap_patterns.description: Self-action uses HUMBLE form; other's action uses RESPECTFUL form. Inverting is wrong.
trap_patterns.wrong_example: せんせい、いただきますか？ (asking teacher to eat)
trap_patterns.correct_example: せんせい、めしあがりますか？
trap_patterns.defense: Self → humble (まいる/いたす/いただく). Other → respectful (いらっしゃる/なさる/めしあがる).

trap_patterns.name: moo_vs_mada_pairing
trap_patterns.description: もう pairs with positive past (already V-ed). まだ pairs with ていません (have not V-ed yet).
trap_patterns.wrong_example: もう たべません。 (intent: have not eaten yet)
trap_patterns.correct_example: まだ たべていません。
trap_patterns.defense: もう = already (+positive). まだ = yet (+negative).

trap_patterns.name: to_vs_to_quotative
trap_patterns.description: と as quotative attaches to plain form: 行くと思う, NOT 行きますと思う.
trap_patterns.wrong_example: いきますと おもいます。
trap_patterns.correct_example: いくと おもいます。
trap_patterns.defense: Subordinate-clause-internal verbs are PLAIN form.

trap_patterns.name: ageru_vs_kureru_direction
trap_patterns.description: あげる = I/X give TO Y. くれる = Y gives TO me/in-group.
trap_patterns.wrong_example: ははが プレゼントを あげました。 (mother gave to me)
trap_patterns.correct_example: ははが プレゼントを くれました。
trap_patterns.defense: Direction matters: to-me = くれる. From-me-or-outgroup = あげる.

trap_patterns.name: iie_vs_iiyo
trap_patterns.description: いい (good) vs いいえ (no). One mora difference.
trap_patterns.wrong_example: いい です — declining offer
trap_patterns.defense: When refusing an offer: いいえ + けっこう. When agreeing it's good: いい (no preceding え).

trap_patterns.name: kaeru_homophones
trap_patterns.description: かえる has 3 different kanji: 帰る (return), 変える (change), 買える (can buy).
trap_patterns.correct_example: うちに かえる。/ いろを かえる。
trap_patterns.defense: Distinguish by context.

trap_patterns.name: hashi_pitch_minimal
trap_patterns.description: はし has 2 readings + 3 kanji: 橋 (bridge, LH), 箸 (chopsticks, HL), 端 (edge, LH).
trap_patterns.defense: Pitch matters in listening sections.

trap_patterns.name: ame_pitch_minimal
trap_patterns.description: あめ has 2 pitches: 雨 (rain, HL) vs 飴 (candy, LH).
trap_patterns.defense: Pitch matters.

trap_patterns.name: yasui_kanji_distinction
trap_patterns.description: やすい = 安い (cheap) AND 易しい (easy). Different kanji, same reading.
trap_patterns.correct_example: Context: price → 安い; difficulty → 易しい.
trap_patterns.defense: Use context.

trap_patterns.name: atsui_kanji_distinction
trap_patterns.description: あつい = 暑い (hot weather) vs 熱い (hot object). Different kanji.
trap_patterns.defense: Weather → 暑い; object → 熱い.

trap_patterns.name: person_counter_irregular
trap_patterns.description: 1-person is ひとり (not いちにん). 2-people is ふたり (not ににん). 3+ regular (さんにん, よにん).
trap_patterns.wrong_example: いちにん きました
trap_patterns.defense: Memorize: ひとり / ふたり / さんにん / よにん.

trap_patterns.name: 4_reading_yo_yon_shi
trap_patterns.description: 4 has 3 readings: よ (4時 よじ), よん (general), し (4月 しがつ). 7 similar: しち (時/月) / なな (general).
trap_patterns.defense: 4時=よじ, 4月=しがつ, 4本=よんほん. Memorize per-counter.

trap_patterns.name: right_left_mirror
trap_patterns.description: 右 starts with SHORT ノ, 左 starts with LONG horizontal — only stroke 1 distinguishes them visually.
trap_patterns.wrong_example: Wrong stroke order produces unbalanced character.
trap_patterns.defense: 右 → ノ first. 左 → horizontal first.

trap_patterns.name: ten_versus_dai_dot
trap_patterns.description: 大 vs 太 vs 犬: dot position differs. 大=no dot. 太=dot below crossing. 犬=dot top-right.
trap_patterns.defense: Memorize dot-position for the three.

trap_patterns.name: box_kanji_close_last
trap_patterns.description: Box-closure: enclosure kanji (口/日/田/月/国/車/西) write the bottom-closing horizontal LAST, after the inside.
trap_patterns.correct_example: Outline first, fill inside, close at bottom.
trap_patterns.defense: Universal box-closure rule.

trap_patterns.name: shinnyou_radical_last
trap_patterns.description: 辶 (path-radical) in 道/週 is written LAST, after all other components.
trap_patterns.defense: Universal for 辶 kanji.

trap_patterns.name: scan_vs_read_choice
trap_patterns.description: Mondai 4-5 (短文/中文): read for understanding. Mondai 6 (情報検索): SCAN, don't read.
trap_patterns.defense: Match strategy to mondai type.

trap_patterns.name: register_signal_mismatch
trap_patterns.description: Conversation passages use polite ます/です. Diary uses plain だ. Letter has formal closing.
trap_patterns.correct_example: Use register cues to identify text type.
trap_patterns.defense: Spot copula form (です/だ/でございます).

trap_patterns.name: discourse_marker_attention
trap_patterns.description: Watch でも/しかし for contrast (changes direction). から/ので signal reason. それから/そして signal sequence.
trap_patterns.defense: Highlight markers mentally.

trap_patterns.name: mentioned_but_rejected
trap_patterns.description: Mondai 1: things mentioned in audio are often REJECTED. Wait for the FINAL decision.
trap_patterns.correct_example: Listen for でも/じゃ/そうですか change-of-mind signals.
trap_patterns.defense: The FINAL decision wins.

trap_patterns.name: similar_sound_confusion
trap_patterns.description: あした (tomorrow) vs あさって (day after tomorrow). たべる vs たべない. Listen carefully for negation morae.
trap_patterns.defense: Focus on suffix morae for negation.

trap_patterns.name: speaker_swap
trap_patterns.description: When two speakers, action of A is often attributed to B in distractors. Track WHO does what.
trap_patterns.defense: Mental notation: M=man, F=woman.

trap_patterns.name: number_close_distractor
trap_patterns.description: Times/numbers: distractors often differ by 1 (3時/4時, 100円/110円). Listen for exact morae.
trap_patterns.defense: Count morae carefully.

trap_patterns.name: register_mismatch_response
trap_patterns.description: Mondai 4 (immediate response): wrong responses often use mismatched register (casual to senior etc.)
trap_patterns.wrong_example: うん in response to teacher.
trap_patterns.defense: Polite-context → polite response.

techniques.name: skip_and_return
techniques.description: If a question takes >2x your average time, mark it and move on. Return at the end.
techniques.rationale: JLPT is point-per-question; do not lose easy points by getting stuck on a hard one.
techniques.warning: Listening: CANNOT skip — audio plays once.

techniques.name: elimination_first
techniques.description: Eliminate 1-2 wrong answers first; even if unsure, guess from remaining 2.

techniques.name: pre_read_listening
techniques.title_ja: 聴解：先に選択肢を読む
techniques.description: For mondai 1-2 (where prompt + choices are printed): read them BEFORE audio plays.
techniques.rationale: Knowing what to listen for primes your attention.

techniques.name: particle_form_first
techniques.title_ja: 助詞・活用形を先に確認
techniques.description: For grammar mondai: the answer is usually FORCED by the surrounding particles/forms. Identify the constraint before reading choices.

techniques.name: star_anchor_composition
techniques.description: For mondai 2 (★ position): build the sentence backwards from constraint clauses; don't try to assemble linearly.
techniques.rationale: Many compositions have ONE structurally-forced order; find it via context-clauses first.

techniques.name: scan_dont_read
techniques.description: Mondai 6 of bunpou/dokkai: scan visually (numerics, headers, days). Do NOT read linearly.
techniques.rationale: Format is notice/menu/schedule — designed for scanning.

techniques.name: final_decision_wins
techniques.description: Mondai 1: the speaker often proposes A, gets rejected, then agrees to B. The answer is B (the final decision).
techniques.rationale: Common N5 trap: pick what was mentioned FIRST.

techniques.name: guess_no_negative_marking
techniques.description: JLPT has NO negative marking for wrong answers. Always fill an answer — even if you guess randomly.
techniques.rationale: Expected value of random guess = +0.25 points. Expected value of blank = 0.

techniques.name: context_disambiguation
techniques.description: If a word has multiple meanings, look at surrounding context to identify the intended one.
techniques.rationale: Many distractors use the wrong meaning of a multi-meaning word.

techniques.name: track_two_speakers
techniques.description: When two speakers, mentally label as M (male) and F (female), and tag each piece of info.
techniques.rationale: Common trap: confuse speaker A's action with speaker B's.

techniques.name: register_match_response
techniques.description: For mondai 3-4 chokai: pick the response that matches the social relationship (junior-senior, customer-staff, friend-friend).

techniques.name: time_pacing_per_mondai
techniques.description: Allocate time per mondai upfront. Stop if a mondai is taking >120% of its budget.
techniques.rationale: Avoid spending 5 min on a 20-second mondai 1 kanji-reading question.

techniques.name: mark_for_review_pencil_mark
techniques.description: Mark uncertain answers with a small dot; review only those in the final 5 min.

techniques.name: answer_sheet_block_fill
techniques.title_ja: 解答用紙：まとめて塗る
techniques.description: Mark answers in the test booklet first, then transfer to answer sheet every 10 questions. Reduces errors and saves time.
techniques.rationale: Constant switching between problem and answer sheet wastes time.

techniques.name: kanji_distractor_kana_check
techniques.title_ja: 漢字読み：かなの違いを確認
techniques.description: Mondai 1 (kanji reading): distractors often differ by one mora (こう/ごう, しゃ/じゃ, etc.). Check kana letter-by-letter.

score_breakdown.scoring_system: JEES post-2010 scoring. Scaled (NOT raw point totals).

language_knowledge_and_reading_combined.label: Lang Knowledge (Vocab + Grammar) + Reading
language_knowledge_and_reading_combined.note: These three are scaled together. Individual component score is NOT broken out.

total_score.rule: ALL section minimums must be met (38 + 19 = 57 minimum from both) AND total must be >=80.

score_breakdown.scaling_explanation: Raw scores are scaled by section difficulty using equipercentile mapping. Raw 60% does NOT necessarily map to scaled 60%. Historically, raw ~50% has been needed for the section minimums.

diagnostic_band.below_38_or_19: fail (any section below minimum = fail regardless of total)
diagnostic_band.38_57: weak — needs focused review of section topics

diagnostic_questions: Quiz: 5 particle-fill questions covering は/が/を/に/で/と/から/まで/も
diagnostic_areas.drill_recommendation: If <60% correct: review n5-002 to n5-013 grammar patterns + Vocab Batch V8 counter_register entries.

diagnostic_questions: Conjugate 食べる, 行く, する across te-form, past, negative
diagnostic_areas.drill_recommendation: If <60% correct: drill wrong_corrected_pair entries on n5-067 (Vた), n5-069 (Vて), n5-066 (Vない).

diagnostic_questions: Conjugate 高い (i-Adj) and しずか (na-Adj) across negative, past, te-form
diagnostic_areas.drill_recommendation: If <60% correct: review n5-079 to n5-089 patterns.

diagnostic_questions: Quiz: count 3 people, 4 books, 1 cup, 5 long objects
diagnostic_areas.drill_recommendation: If <60% correct: review V8 counter_register entries + n5-108 to n5-114 grammar patterns.

diagnostic_questions: Recognize readings of 大学, 学校, 日本, 食べる, 行く
diagnostic_areas.drill_recommendation: If <60% correct: review on_kun_pair_drill field for each problem kanji.

diagnostic_questions: Distinguish 右/左, 入/出, 大/犬/太
diagnostic_areas.drill_recommendation: If <60% correct: review stroke_order_trap and lookalikes fields.

diagnostic_questions: Pick appropriate response for: teacher-student vs friend-friend contexts
diagnostic_areas.drill_recommendation: If <60% correct: review politeness_ladder on all grammar patterns + honorific_chain on verbs.

diagnostic_questions: Listen to 3 items at standard N5 rate (180-230 mora/min); track correctness
diagnostic_areas.drill_recommendation: If <60% correct: use audio_slow versions on listening items. Build up to standard rate.

diagnostic_questions: Read 2 short passages with でも/しかし/から/ので; answer about cause-effect
diagnostic_areas.drill_recommendation: If <60% correct: review n5-121..134 grammar patterns + dokkai discourse_markers_used.

drill_methodology: Step 1: Take a 5-question diagnostic per area (timed).
Step 2: For areas <60% correct, review the linked module's curated content (wrong_corrected_pair entries are the highest-yield drill source).
Step 3: Re-take the 5-question diagnostic after each review session.
Step 4: Move to the next area when target accuracy is 80%+.

five_minute_summary: (1) JLPT N5 has TWO sections: combined Lang+Reading (60 min, 49 q, min 38/120) AND Listening (30 min, 24 q, min 19/60).
(2) Pass = both sections at minimum AND total >=80/180.
(3) Mondai have specific strategies — review them before exam.
(4) Time discipline: skip-and-return for Section 1; cannot skip in Listening.
(5) Always answer — no negative marking. Random guess = +25% expected value.
(6) Trap awareness: particle-misuse, kanji homophones, mentioned-but-rejected listening, register mismatch — all are explicit distractors.
(7) Audio plays ONCE. Pre-read printed prompts. Mental-tag speakers in 2-person dialogues.

study_distribution_recommendation.chokai: 25% — daily 30 min listening practice with native-rate items
study_distribution_recommendation.test_simulation: 5% — full timed mock exam every 2 weeks

two_week_drill_schedule.focus: Half-length timed mock (3 mondai of each section)
Identify weak areas from mock; drill

exam_day_checklist: Bring HB pencils (NOT mechanical) and an eraser.
Bring photo ID and admission slip.
Arrive 30 min early. Bathroom break before each section.
Pace yourself — don't sprint mondai 1, don't crawl mondai 6.
Trust your first instinct unless you find concrete evidence against it.
Keep moving — never linger more than 2x average per question.
Fill EVERY blank — never leave any answer empty.

ui_integration_pointers.score_estimator: Aggregate user's session correctness rate; map to scaled-score band using diagnostic_band table.
ui_integration_pointers.weak_area_dashboard: Cross-reference user's wrong-answer history against trap_patterns and diagnostic_drills to suggest review paths.
ui_integration_pointers.timer_overlay: Per-mondai timer using section_timing.*.mondai_breakdown[*].seconds_per_q. Yellow when 75% used, red when 100% used.
ui_integration_pointers.strategy_card_modal: Show technique cards by id (T3-001 to T3-015) on hover/tap, contextual to current mondai.
ui_integration_pointers.exam_day_prep: Show 5-minute summary + checklist 24h before user's registered exam date.
